pattern: In a directed acyclic graph, a vertex can only be reached from elsewhere if something points at it, so every vertex with no incoming edge must anchor its own component of the required set.
hints: Think about which vertices could possibly be left out of the answer: only ones that some other vertex can reach.
Count how many edges point into each vertex; a vertex nothing points at can never be supplied by another starting point.
The answer is exactly the set of vertices whose in-degree is zero — collect them in one pass over the edge list, no traversal needed.

edge_inputs.description: one root feeds a small fan of descendants
two disjoint chains, each needs its own root
minimum size: two nodes, one edge
star graph, one hub reaches every other node directly
diamond re-convergence: node 3 has two parents but is still not a root
long single chain: only the very first node qualifies
isolated vertices untouched by any edge must still be included
three separate roots feeding a mix of private and shared descendants

stress.description: random DAGs built over a shuffled topological order so no cycle can form, edge count capped for fast oracle verification
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 400))
    order = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(order)
    max_possible = n * (n - 1) // 2
    edge_count = min(max_possible, max(1, n * 2))
    edges = set()
    guard = 0
    while len(edges) < edge_count and guard < edge_count * 8:
        i = rng.randint(0, n - 2)
        j = rng.randint(i + 1, n - 1)
        u, v = order[i], order[j]
        edges.add((u, v))
        guard += 1
    return [n, [list(e) for e in edges]]
stress.note: edges only ever go from an earlier position to a later position in the shuffled order, guaranteeing acyclicity
